bin-wrbulk-001.text: 68-year-old male (was 51 at time of visit) presenting for routine eye exam. Visual acuity 20/25 OU, mild refractive error. Early nuclear sclerotic cataract changes noted bilaterally. No diabetic retinopathy. Return in 12 months for surveillance.

bin-wrbulk-002.text: Annual skin check. Several actinic keratoses noted on forearms and scalp, treated with cryotherapy today. No suspicious pigmented lesions identified. Sun protection counseling provided. Return in 1 year.

bin-wrbulk-003.text: Diabetic foot exam performed. Monofilament sensation intact bilaterally, pedal pulses palpable, no ulceration or skin breakdown. Nails trimmed. Continue daily foot checks at home.

bin-wrbulk-004.text: Routine cleaning and exam. Two small carious lesions identified and restored with composite filling. Gingiva mildly inflamed, oral hygiene instruction reviewed. Recall in 6 months.

bin-wrbulk-005.text: Audiogram shows mild bilateral high-frequency sensorineural hearing loss. Discussed hearing aid options with patient. Follow-up scheduled for fitting.

bin-wrbulk-006.text: Follow-up for benign prostatic hyperplasia. PSA 1.9 ng/mL, stable from prior. Mild lower urinary tract symptoms, well controlled on tamsulosin. Continue current regimen, recheck PSA in 1 year.

bin-wrbulk-007.text: Cataract surveillance visit. Vision 20/30 OD, 20/25 OS, mild progression of nuclear sclerosis. Not yet visually significant. Continue annual monitoring.

bin-wrbulk-008.text: Follow-up for plantar fasciitis, right foot. Symptoms markedly improved with stretching program and OTC orthotic inserts. Discharged from active podiatric care, return PRN.

bin-wrbulk-009.text: Annual skin check. Single benign-appearing nevus on right upper back, stable in size and appearance compared to photographs from 2018. No biopsy indicated. Return in 1 year.

bin-wrbulk-010.text: Routine visit to review annual labs. Lipid panel and renal function stable. A1c mildly elevated at 7.1%, discussed diet and adherence. No medication changes made today.

bin-wrbulk-011.text: Hearing aid check and reprogramming. Patient reports good satisfaction with current amplification settings in most environments. No further adjustments needed today.

bin-wrbulk-012.text: Reviewed low-dose CT chest performed for lung cancer screening given remote 20 pack-year smoking history. No suspicious nodules, Lung-RADS 1. Continue annual screening per guidelines.

bin-wrbulk-ext-001.text: Referral letter dated 8/5/2009 requesting routine ophthalmologic evaluation for a 51-year-old male with no known eye disease. Family history noncontributory. Please evaluate and advise.

bin-wrbulk-ext-002.text: Referral letter dated 6/20/2012 requesting dermatologic evaluation of sun-damaged skin, multiple actinic keratoses on forearms and scalp. History of significant outdoor occupational sun exposure (electrician).

bin-wrbulk-ext-003.text: Transferred dental records summary: patient has been a regular biannual patient since 1995. History of routine restorative work, no periodontal disease. No outstanding treatment needs at time of transfer.

bin-wrbulk-ext-004.text: Prior authorization letter dated 7/5/2016 approving outpatient esophagogastroduodenoscopy for evaluation of chronic GERD symptoms. Authorization valid for 90 days from issue date.

bin-wrbulk-ext-005.text: Referral letter dated 1/20/2017 requesting formal audiometric evaluation for reported difficulty hearing in group settings and during phone calls.

bin-wrbulk-ext-006.text: Outside consult summary dated 5/28/2018 for evaluation of urinary hesitancy and weak stream. Impression: benign prostatic hyperplasia. Recommended starting tamsulosin and routine PSA monitoring.

bin-wrbulk-ext-007.text: Outside orthopedic consult dated 9/30/2021. Mild-to-moderate osteoarthritic changes on knee X-ray. Recommended conservative management with activity modification and PRN corticosteroid injection.

bin-wrbulk-ext-008.text: Prior authorization letter dated 2/11/2022 approving ophthalmology consultation and surgical planning for cataract evaluation, right eye.
